title: Charred Egyptian Almond Flour Flatbread
description: A crispy, flavorful flatbread perfect for post-workout recovery, made with almond flour, cure, and a hint of Egyptian spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C) with a cast-iron skillet or baking stone inside.
In a large bowl, whisk together almond flour, coconut flour, eggs, water, salt, black pepper, cumin, paprika, and cure until smooth.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it forms a cohesive ball.
Divide the dough into 2-4 equal pieces, depending on desired flatbread size.
Shape each piece into a ball and flatten slightly into a disk.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-high heat and brush with olive oil.
Cook each flatbread for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and slightly charred.
Transfer the flatbreads to the preheated oven and bake for 5-7 minutes, or until crispy and golden.
Remove from oven and let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Optional: sprinkle with sesame seeds before serving.
